steps.title: Find the main subject
steps.content: The main subject is Blaise Pascal's Pascaline device, a singular noun phrase. The long phrase between dashes is parenthetical, so it does not change the number of the subject.

steps.title: Choose the singular verb
steps.content: Choice A is a participle and cannot serve as the main finite verb. Choice B is plural past tense, and choice D is plural present tense. The sentence needs a singular past-tense verb to agree with device and fit the historical context. The answer is C.
